sentences.formal: This matter was carried forward to the next meeting.
sentences.informal: Can you carry that for a sec?

words.usageNote: 'Carry this' = transport specific thing. 'Carry forward' = bring to next phase. 'Carry weight' = have influence.

words.simpleMeaning: to let something fall or to stop doing

sentences.office: Drop this idea for now.
sentences.interview: I had to drop this plan due to challenges.
sentences.business: Those sales dropped last quarter.
sentences.formal: This course was dropped from the curriculum.

words.usageNote: 'Drop this' = let fall or abandon. 'Drop off this' = deliver. 'Drop this topic' = stop discussing it.

words.simpleMeaning: to rotate or to change direction

sentences.office: This project turned out well.
sentences.interview: I want to turn this opportunity into a success.
sentences.business: Those losses turned into profits.
sentences.formal: This situation has turned critical.

words.usageNote: 'Turn this way' = direction. 'Turn on/off this' = switch. 'Turn this into' = transform. 'It's your turn' = your chance.

words.simpleMeaning: to examine or verify something

sentences.office: Check this report for errors.
sentences.interview: I check this kind of detail carefully.
sentences.business: Those invoices must be checked.
sentences.formal: This has been checked and verified.

words.usageNote: 'Check this' = verify specific thing. 'Check in on this' = review progress. 'Check out that' = look at something interesting.

words.simpleMeaning: to locate or discover something

sentences.office: I found this solution.
sentences.interview: Where did you find this information?
sentences.business: Those clients found this useful.
sentences.formal: This information was found during the audit.

words.usageNote: 'Find this' = locate specific thing. 'Find out about this' = discover information. 'I find this interesting' = think/feel.

words.simpleMeaning: to not be able to find something

sentences.office: This data was lost in the crash.
sentences.interview: I never lose this opportunity.
sentences.business: Those sales were lost to competitors.
sentences.formal: This information must not be lost.

words.usageNote: 'Lose this' = misplace specific thing. 'Lose at this' = fail in competition. 'Lose sight of this' = forget the goal.

words.simpleMeaning: to achieve victory or success

sentences.office: This strategy helped us win.
sentences.interview: I want to win this opportunity.
sentences.business: Those bids were won last quarter.
sentences.formal: This contract was won through competitive bidding.

words.usageNote: 'Win this' = be victorious in specific contest. 'Win over this person' = convince them. 'This is a win' = positive outcome.

words.simpleMeaning: to fail to keep or win

sentences.office: We lost that client last month.
sentences.interview: I've never lost this kind of opportunity.
sentences.business: Those customers were lost due to poor service.
sentences.formal: This account was lost due to pricing issues.

words.usageNote: 'Lose this' in competition. 'Lose + noun' (not 'loose'). 'This is a losing battle' = unwinnable situation.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep from danger or to store for future use

sentences.office: This shortcut saves a lot of time.
sentences.interview: I saved this company from a major setback.
sentences.business: Those efforts saved us significant costs.
sentences.formal: This data has been saved and backed up.

words.usageNote: 'Save this' = store or rescue. 'Save this file' = digital saving. 'Save for this' = set aside money. 'This saves time' = reduces effort.

words.simpleMeaning: to divide and give portions to others

sentences.office: Share this document with the team.
sentences.interview: Let me share this experience.
sentences.business: Those ideas were shared in the brainstorm session.
sentences.formal: This information has been shared with all stakeholders.

words.usageNote: 'Share this' = give to others. 'Share on social media' = post online. 'This share' (finance) = a portion of company stock.

words.simpleMeaning: to agree to receive or take something

sentences.office: Accept this offer.
sentences.interview: I would be happy to accept this position.
sentences.business: Those terms were accepted by both parties.
sentences.formal: This proposal has been formally accepted.

words.usageNote: 'Accept this offer/invitation' = agree to receive. 'Accept this fact' = acknowledge reality. 'Accepted' = approved.

words.simpleMeaning: to not accept something

sentences.office: This proposal was rejected.
sentences.interview: I was rejected from this company once.
sentences.business: Those defective products were rejected.
sentences.formal: This application has been formally rejected.
sentences.informal: Sorry, I have to reject that!

words.usageNote: 'Reject this' = say no to specific thing. 'This was rejected' = passive. 'Rejection' from a company = not getting selected.

words.simpleMeaning: to have the same opinion as someone else

sentences.office: Do you agree with this plan?
sentences.interview: I completely agree with this approach.
sentences.business: Those stakeholders agreed with this strategy.
sentences.formal: This has been agreed upon by all parties.
sentences.informal: Yeah, I totally agree with that!

words.usageNote: 'Agree with this' = same opinion. 'Agree on this' = reach consensus. 'This is agreed' = settled/decided.

words.simpleMeaning: to have a different opinion

sentences.office: I disagree with this decision.
sentences.interview: I respectfully disagree with this assessment.
sentences.business: Those partners disagreed on this point.
sentences.formal: This view is one that I disagree with.

words.usageNote: 'Disagree with this/that' = object to. 'Respectfully disagree' = polite opposition. 'This is a disagreement' = conflict of opinion.

words.simpleMeaning: to put forward an idea for consideration

sentences.office: This approach was suggested by management.
sentences.interview: I can suggest this improvement.
sentences.business: Those experts suggested this solution.
sentences.formal: This course of action is suggested by the committee.
sentences.informal: I suggest we go with that!

words.usageNote: 'Suggest this' = propose for consideration. 'Suggest + gerund': 'I suggest doing this'. NOT 'suggest to do this'.

words.simpleMeaning: to advise someone to do or choose something

sentences.office: This tool is highly recommended.
sentences.interview: I was recommended for this role.
sentences.business: Those experts recommended this product.
sentences.formal: This course of action is recommended by the board.

words.usageNote: 'Recommend this to someone' = suggest for them. 'I recommend + gerund' not infinitive. 'Highly recommended' = very good.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep away from something

sentences.office: Avoid this mistake.
sentences.interview: I avoid this kind of conflict at work.
sentences.business: Those risks were avoided through planning.
sentences.formal: This risk must be avoided at all costs.

words.usageNote: 'Avoid this' = stay away. 'Avoid + gerund': 'avoid making this mistake'. 'This is unavoidable' = cannot be escaped.

words.simpleMeaning: to think something will happen

sentences.office: This is not what we expected.
sentences.interview: I expect this role to be challenging.
sentences.business: Those clients expected this quality.
sentences.formal: This outcome was expected and planned for.

words.usageNote: 'Expect this' = anticipate. 'This is expected' = predicted/normal. 'Unexpected' = surprising. 'As expected' = as predicted.

words.simpleMeaning: to rely on or be determined by something

sentences.office: This decision depends on the budget.
sentences.interview: My answer depends on this context.
sentences.business: Those results depend on market conditions.
sentences.formal: This outcome depends on multiple factors.

words.usageNote: 'Depend on this' = be influenced by. 'This depends' = varies. 'It depends' = common hedging expression.

words.simpleMeaning: to feel sure that something is true

sentences.office: This is believed to be the best approach.
sentences.interview: I believe this role is perfect for me.
sentences.business: Those customers believe in this brand.
sentences.formal: This claim is widely believed to be true.

words.usageNote: 'Believe this' = accept as true. 'Believe in this' = have faith. 'I believe that...' = opinion marker.

words.simpleMeaning: to demonstrate the truth of something

sentences.office: This result proves our theory.
sentences.interview: I will prove this through my work.
sentences.business: Those numbers prove this product's success.
sentences.formal: This hypothesis has been proven.

words.usageNote: 'Prove this' = demonstrate truth. 'Proven/proved' both acceptable. 'This proves that...' = evidence.

words.simpleMeaning: to feel sad about the absence of something

sentences.office: Don't miss this deadline.
sentences.interview: I miss this kind of teamwork.
sentences.business: Those clients missed this offer.
sentences.formal: This opportunity was missed.

words.usageNote: 'Miss this place/person' = feel sad about absence. 'Miss this deadline/bus' = fail to catch. 'Don't miss this' = attend/catch it.

words.simpleMeaning: to find pleasure in something

sentences.office: I enjoyed this project.
sentences.interview: I enjoy this type of work.
sentences.business: Those clients enjoyed this experience.
sentences.formal: This experience has been thoroughly enjoyed.

words.usageNote: 'Enjoy this' = find pleasure in specific thing. 'Enjoy + gerund': 'enjoy doing this'. 'Did you enjoy that?' = past experience question.

words.simpleMeaning: an official written paper

sentences.office: This document needs approval.
sentences.interview: I will bring this document tomorrow.
sentences.business: Those documents were reviewed by the lawyer.
sentences.formal: This document is legally binding.
sentences.informal: Where did I put that document?

words.usageNote: 'This document' = specific paper. 'Document this' = record in writing. 'Documentation' = the process or set of documents.

words.simpleMeaning: a written account of something

sentences.office: Submit this report by Monday.
sentences.interview: I prepared this report single-handedly.
sentences.business: Those reports show positive growth.
sentences.formal: This report has been compiled by the audit team.

words.usageNote: 'Report this issue' = notify about it. 'This report covers...' = content. 'Report to this person' = give updates to.

words.simpleMeaning: a gathering for discussion

sentences.office: That meeting was very productive.
sentences.interview: I attended this kind of meeting regularly.
sentences.business: Those meetings brought new clients.
sentences.formal: This meeting has been scheduled for 3 PM.

words.usageNote: 'This meeting' = specific current meeting. 'Meeting minutes' = written record. 'Meeting someone' = introduction vs 'meeting' (gathering).

words.simpleMeaning: a planned piece of work with a specific goal

sentences.office: That project was very successful.
sentences.interview: I led this project from start to finish.
sentences.business: Those projects are on track.
sentences.formal: This project has been approved by the board.

words.usageNote: 'Project this idea' = present visually or share. 'This project is on track' = going well. 'Project manager' = leads projects.

words.simpleMeaning: a group working together toward a goal

sentences.office: That team completed it on time.
sentences.interview: This team environment suits me.
sentences.business: Those teams performed excellently.
sentences.formal: This team has been awarded for excellence.

words.usageNote: 'Team' is treated as singular in American English, plural in British. 'This team works well together' vs 'this team are working hard'.

words.simpleMeaning: a business organization

sentences.office: That company is our competitor.
sentences.interview: I want to grow with this company.
sentences.business: Those companies are our partners.
sentences.formal: This company was founded in 1990.
sentences.informal: This company has the best perks!

sentences.informal: This question has no right answer!

words.usageNote: 'This question is tricky' = hard. 'Question this' = challenge it. 'Out of this question' = impossible. 'Good question!'

words.simpleMeaning: a response to a question or problem

sentences.office: Give this answer.
sentences.interview: My answer to this is clear.
sentences.business: Those answers satisfied clients.
sentences.formal: This answer has been officially provided.

words.usageNote: 'This is the right answer' = correct. 'Answer to this question' = response. 'No answer for this' = unresolved.

words.simpleMeaning: a matter dealt with in a text or discussion

sentences.office: Stay on this topic.
sentences.interview: This topic is relevant to the role.
sentences.business: Those topics were covered in training.
sentences.formal: This topic has been included in the agenda.
sentences.informal: This topic is so hot right now!

words.usageNote: 'This topic is off-limits' = cannot discuss. 'Stick to this topic' = stay focused. 'Change this topic' = switch subject.

words.simpleMeaning: a topic being discussed or studied

sentences.office: Change the subject.
sentences.interview: This subject interests me deeply.
sentences.business: Those subjects are covered in training.
sentences.formal: This subject has been raised officially.

words.usageNote: 'This subject matter' = specific topic. 'Subject to this rule' = governed by it. 'This is a sensitive subject' = emotional topic.

words.simpleMeaning: a method of communication using words

sentences.office: This language skill is needed.
sentences.interview: I speak this language fluently.
sentences.business: Those language barriers were overcome.
sentences.formal: This language has official status.

words.usageNote: 'This language has unique features' = characteristics. 'Language barrier in this context' = communication difficulty. 'Body language here'.

words.simpleMeaning: the way words sound when spoken

sentences.office: Correct this pronunciation.
sentences.interview: I worked hard on this pronunciation.
sentences.business: Those pronunciation errors were noted.
sentences.formal: This pronunciation guide was issued.
sentences.informal: I can't get this pronunciation right!

words.usageNote: 'This pronunciation sounds native' = natural. 'Work on this pronunciation' = practice. 'IPA for this word' = phonetic notation.

words.simpleMeaning: the act of paying attention to what is said

sentences.office: Practice this active listening.
sentences.interview: Listening to this feedback helped me.
sentences.business: Those listening sessions revealed needs.
sentences.formal: This listening test has been scheduled.

words.usageNote: 'Active listening in this conversation' = attentive. 'Listening skill for this language' = comprehension. 'Good at listening to this'.

words.simpleMeaning: the act of communicating through words

sentences.office: Public speaking in this role is required.
sentences.interview: My speaking skills are strong.
sentences.business: Those speaking opportunities build presence.
sentences.formal: This speaking engagement is confirmed.

words.usageNote: 'Speaking on this topic' = talking about. 'Public speaking for this role' = presenting to audiences. 'Speaking fluency in this'.

words.simpleMeaning: looking at and understanding written text

sentences.office: Complete this reading assignment.
sentences.interview: This reading about the company helped.
sentences.business: Those reading habits keep me sharp.
sentences.formal: This reading material was provided.

words.usageNote: 'Reading this document' = studying it. 'Reading comprehension for this language' = understanding ability. 'Reading list for this subject'.

words.simpleMeaning: the activity of putting words on paper or screen

sentences.office: Improve this writing style.
sentences.interview: My writing skills are strong.
sentences.business: Those writing standards matter.
sentences.formal: This writing has been submitted formally.

words.usageNote: 'This writing style' = how someone writes. 'Writing on the wall about this' = signs of trouble. 'Write about this topic' = create content.

words.simpleMeaning: repeated exercise to improve skill

sentences.office: Practice this skill daily.
sentences.interview: This practice made me confident.
sentences.business: Those practice runs prepared us.
sentences.formal: This practice is recommended.

words.usageNote: 'Practice this daily' = repetitive exercise. 'Practice what you preach about this' = do as you say. 'Best practice for this' = recommended approach.

words.simpleMeaning: an action that is wrong or incorrect

sentences.office: Don't repeat this mistake.
sentences.interview: I learned from this mistake.
sentences.business: Those mistakes cost money.
sentences.formal: This mistake has been noted.

words.usageNote: 'Make this mistake' = err in this way. 'Correct this mistake' = fix it. 'This is my mistake' = taking responsibility.

words.simpleMeaning: something learned from experience or teaching

sentences.office: This is a lesson for everyone.
sentences.interview: This lesson made me better.
sentences.business: Those lessons shaped strategy.
sentences.formal: This lesson has been incorporated in training.

words.usageNote: 'Learn this lesson' = take the moral. 'This lesson taught me...' = what was gained. 'Lesson of this story' = moral.

words.simpleMeaning: guidance offered to someone

sentences.office: Follow this advice.
sentences.interview: This advice helped me prepare.
sentences.business: Those expert advisors gave this advice.
sentences.formal: This advice has been formally given.

words.usageNote: 'This advice is gold' = very valuable. 'Take this advice' = follow it. 'A piece of advice about this' = one suggestion.

words.simpleMeaning: the point in time at which something starts

sentences.office: This is the beginning of a new chapter.
sentences.interview: This role is the beginning of my dream career.
sentences.business: Those beginnings were modest.
sentences.formal: This is the beginning of the fiscal year.
sentences.informal: This is just the beginning — wait and watch!

words.usageNote: 'This is only the beginning' = much more to come. 'Beginning of this era' = start of period. 'In the beginning of this' = at the start.

words.simpleMeaning: the final point of something

sentences.office: This is the end of the fiscal year.
sentences.interview: At the end of this interview, I was confident.
sentences.business: Those projects came to an end.
sentences.formal: This is the end of the formal session.

words.usageNote: 'This is the end of...' = final point. 'In the end, this worked out' = ultimately. 'End of this story' = conclusion. 'Never-ending this'.

words.simpleMeaning: travel from one place to another or a process over time

sentences.office: This career journey is rewarding.
sentences.interview: This journey has prepared me well.
sentences.business: Those journey milestones were key.
sentences.formal: This journey of transformation continues.

words.usageNote: 'This journey is long' = takes time. 'Life journey' = path through life. 'This journey of learning' = educational process. 'Begin this journey'.
